Title: Interactions of WRKY15 and WRKY33 transcription factors and their roles in the resistance of oilseed rape to Sclerotinia infection

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytoalexin contributing to resistance against necrotrophic pathogens including Sclerotinia sclerotiorum
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in roots and leaves; lowest in stems and seedlings; moderate in flowers, siliques and seeds; induced by Sclerotinia sclerotiorum infection and salicylic acid
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed transgenically in leaves of Westar; promoter-GUS active mainly in young leaves and roots and induced around infection sites and by SA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sclerotinia sclerotiorum infection; salicylic acid treatment
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: camalexin biosynthesis and associated defense hormone-responsive pathways
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAD3; CYP71A13; PR1; PR5; PDF1.2; PR3; PR4; BnWRKY33 (self-activation)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via binding W-box elements in its own promoter and promoting downstream defense/camalexin genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates PAD3 and CYP71A13; upregulates SA-responsive PR1 and PR5 and JA-responsive PDF1.2, PR3 and PR4; activates its own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Sclerotinia sclerotiorum infection; salicylic acid; repression/competition by BnWRKY15; discussion notes activation by MPK3/MPK6 analogy from Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates resistance to Sclerotinia infection and promotes expression of camalexin synthesis-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression in Brassica napus; qRT-PCR of downstream genes; promoter-GUS assays; EMSA; Arabidopsis protoplast dual-luciferase assays; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnWRKY33 is induced by Sclerotinia sclerotiorum and salicylic acid. 2. Overexpression in B. napus increases resistance to Sclerotinia and elevates PAD3 and CYP71A13 expression, linking it to camalexin biosynthesis. 3. BnWRKY33 binds W-box elements in its own promoter and activates its own transcription, forming a positive feedback loop. 4. It also enhances SA- and JA-responsive defense gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive autoregulatory feedback through self-binding/self-activation of the BnWRKY33 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator and likely key inducible activator linking pathogen response to camalexin-associated defense output
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: double CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression; native promoter and truncated promoters for reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mapped to linkage group A05; binds all three W-boxes in the responsive promoter region, though the first W-box is indispensable for promoter activation in reporter assays; BnWRKY15 suppresses its transactivation ability and competes for promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful positive regulatory gene for engineering enhanced Sclerotinia resistance in rapeseed, potentially in combination with camalexin pathway genes, but regulation should be balanced to avoid excessive defense activation costs

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytoalexin contributing to resistance against pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group IId WRKY transcription factor; WRKY family; contains transcriptional repression domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in roots, young leaves and siliques; induced by Sclerotinia sclerotiorum and H2O2; reduced by salicylic acid
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed transgenically in Westar; promoter-GUS stronger in roots, young leaves and pod epidermis; induced in rosette leaves by Sclerotinia and H2O2
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sclerotinia sclerotiorum infection; H2O2 treatment; salicylic acid treatment
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: negative modulation of camalexin-associated defense gene expression through repression of BnWRKY33 activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression/competitive binding to W-box region in BnWRKY33 promoter; reduces BnWRKY33 transactivation ability
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses BnWRKY33 expression after infection; lowers PAD3 and CYP71A13 expression indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Sclerotinia sclerotiorum infection; H2O2; salicylic acid negatively affects its expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increases susceptibility to Sclerotinia infection and reduces expression of camalexin biosynthesis-related genes by repressing BnWRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast one-hybrid; overexpression in Brassica napus; qRT-PCR; EMSA; Arabidopsis protoplast dual-luciferase assays; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnWRKY15 binds the Sclerotinia-responsive W-box region in the BnWRKY33 promoter. 2. It is a weak transcriptional repressor localized in the nucleus. 3. Overexpression in B. napus increases susceptibility to Sclerotinia and reduces BnWRKY33, PAD3 and CYP71A13 expression. 4. BnWRKY15 competes with and suppresses the transactivation ability of BnWRKY33.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: functions as a negative modulator of the BnWRKY33 positive feedback circuit
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent negative regulator that limits camalexin-associated defense output by restraining BnWRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: high overexpression may impair growth/seed production in one line (15OE-16 did not grow well and produced no seeds); may reflect defense-growth balance
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression; native promoter for GUS reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Has higher apparent affinity than BnWRKY33 for the shared W-box region in vitro; both proteins bind all three W-boxes, but promoter activation requires the first W-box region
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for downregulation or editing to relieve repression of BnWRKY33 and improve Sclerotinia resistance, with caution regarding defense-growth tradeoffs
